bootstrapNotes: Pure functional core. No I/O in the core; rendering and timing live behind ports. Ruby + RSpec.
missionStatement: Given a Grid of live cells -> return the next Grid by applying Conway's birth/survival rules.
scopeReminder: Advance one generation for a small hard-coded blinker; prove the rule pipeline is wired end to end.

slices.name: walking skeleton: blinker tick
slices.risk: Risk that the rule pipeline (count neighbours -> apply rules -> emit grid) is not wired end to end.
slices.proof: RSpec: a vertical blinker becomes a horizontal blinker after one tick.

slices.name: rule engine handles all 4 cases
slices.risk: Risk that under-population, survival, over-population, and birth are not all covered.
slices.proof: RSpec table-driven specs for each rule cover all neighbour counts 0..8 for live and dead cells.

slices.name: bounded vs. toroidal grid topology
slices.risk: Risk that the neighbour query leaks topology assumptions into the rule engine.
slices.proof: RSpec: swap a BoundedGrid for a ToroidalGrid behind the same port and acceptance still passes.

entities.name: TickRequester
entities.responsibility: Entry point: hands a Grid in, receives NextGrid out.

entities.name: GameOfLife
entities.responsibility: Applies Conway's rules via the neighbour port.

entities.name: NeighbourQuery
entities.responsibility: Counts live neighbours of cell (x,y) in a Grid.

entities.name: BoundedGridAdapter
entities.responsibility: NeighbourQuery for a finite grid; off-grid = dead.

entities.name: Grid
entities.responsibility: Immutable live-cell set with width and height.

entities.name: NextGrid
entities.responsibility: Grid emitted by one tick; same shape, one gen forward.

messages.notes: Pure function: same input grid always yields same next grid; no side effects.
Returns count in 0..8; never raises for in-bounds or out-of-bounds coords (out-of-bounds counted as dead).

items: Does NOT know how cells are stored (array, set, sparse map).
Does NOT know the grid topology (bounded, toroidal, infinite).
Does NOT know Conway's birth/survival rules.
Does NOT mutate or own the Grid; it only answers neighbour counts.
Does NOT know Conway's rules or how to count neighbours.
Does NOT mutate the Grid; it only hands a Grid in and receives [R] NextGrid.
Does NOT know Conway's birth/survival rules.
Does NOT decide a cell's next state; it only answers a count.
Does NOT know Conway's rules; it just holds live-cell coordinates and dimensions.
Does NOT mutate; any change returns a new Grid.
Does NOT know how it was computed; it only carries the resulting Grid state.
Does NOT know about rendering or display; it is a pure data value.

happyPath: ? [C] TickRequester hands a blinker Grid (3 vertical live cells) to [O] GameOfLife.
? [O] GameOfLife asks [P] NeighbourQuery for live-neighbour counts of every relevant cell.
? [A] BoundedGridAdapter answers each count using the real Grid; off-grid cells contribute 0.
? [O] GameOfLife emits [R] NextGrid containing the horizontal blinker (3 horizontal live cells).
failurePath: ? [C] TickRequester hands a Grid with width=0 or height=0 to [O] GameOfLife.
? [O] GameOfLife raises ArgumentError('grid must have positive dimensions'); no [P] NeighbourQuery call is made.
acceptanceTitle: tick advances a vertical blinker to a horizontal blinker through real wiring

risks.risk: Caller skips [O] GameOfLife and mutates Grid directly to 'fix a bug'.
risks.mitigation: Make Grid an immutable frozen value object; expose only Grid.with(live_cells:).

risks.risk: [O] GameOfLife inlines neighbour counting instead of going through [P] NeighbourQuery, leaking bounded-grid assumptions.
risks.mitigation: Acceptance test swaps in a ToroidalGridAdapter and asserts a glider wraps; failure proves the seam is honest.

risks.risk: Rendering / IO sneaks into [O] GameOfLife (printing the grid each tick) and pins it to a console.
risks.mitigation: Keep [O] GameOfLife pure: it returns [R] NextGrid; rendering lives in a separate Caller-side renderer.
